question_title: Will James P.K. Sangma file his nomination as the NPP candidate for Meghalaya's Rajya Sabha seat by the June 8, 2026 deadline?
summary: James P.K. Sangma was officially announced as the MDA-II/NPP candidate for Meghalaya's Rajya Sabha seat on June 3, 2026, and his nomination filing was scheduled for June 5, 2026 at 2:30 PM — the day of this forecast. The deadline is June 8, giving a 3-day buffer. The ruling coalition controls 51 of 60 assembly seats. With the official announcement made, the filing scheduled or already completed, and days of buffer remaining, the probability of YES is ~97%. The only NO scenarios involve extreme tail risks (health emergency, sudden legal disqualification, unprecedented party reversal after formal announcement) which collectively warrant ~3% probability.

factors.description: MDA-II coalition officially announced James P.K. Sangma as candidate on June 3, 2026, at a meeting chaired by CM Conrad Sangma — formal decision already made and publicly reported by multiple credible outlets (EastMojo, Times of India)
Filing was scheduled for today (June 5, 2026) at 2:30 PM — the act of filing is imminent or may have already occurred
3 more days remain until the June 8 deadline, providing ample buffer even if today's scheduled filing was delayed
Small residual risk of health emergency, sudden legal disqualification, or last-minute party reversal after official announcement

reasoning:
**Forecast: 97% probability YES**

James P.K. Sangma will almost certainly file his nomination as the NPP candidate for Meghalaya's Rajya Sabha seat by the June 8 deadline.

**Key evidence:**
- **Official announcement already made**: The MDA-II coalition formally announced James P.K. Sangma as their candidate on June 3, 2026, at a meeting chaired by CM Conrad Sangma
- **Filing scheduled for today**: His nomination papers were expected to be filed at 2:30 PM on June 5, 2026
- **Ample buffer**: Even if today's filing didn't happen, 3 days remain until the June 8 deadline
- **Overwhelming political support**: MDA-II controls 51 of 60 assembly seats (85%)

The ~3% assigned to NO covers extreme tail risks like a sudden health emergency, unexpected legal disqualification, or an unprecedented reversal of a formal coalition decision.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will James P.K. Sangma file his nomination as the NPP candidate for Meghalaya's Rajya Sabha seat by the June 8, 2026 deadline?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43779)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if, on or before the close of the nomination filing window on June 8, 2026 (as set by the Election Commission of India for the Meghalaya Rajya Sabha biennial election), James P.K. Sangma files a nomination paper as a candidate sponsored/endorsed by the National People's Party (NPP) for Meghalaya's lone Rajya Sabha seat, as reflected in the ECI's official list of nominations for that election (https://www.eci.gov.in/) or as reported by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). It resolves NO if no such nomination by James P.K. Sangma is filed by the June 8, 2026 deadline (e.g., NPP nominates a different person, or James files but not as the NPP candidate, or no nomination is filed at all by the deadline).
fine_print:
- "James P.K. Sangma" refers to the Meghalaya politician and former Cabinet Minister, brother of Chief Minister Conrad K. Sangma.
- A nomination counts if it is filed and accepted for record by the Returning Officer by the ECI-stipulated deadline of June 8, 2026, even if it is later withdrawn (by June 11) or rejected at scrutiny (on June 9).
- "NPP candidate" means James P.K. Sangma is shown on the nomination form / ECI list as a candidate set up by the National People's Party (with Form A/B signed by NPP), regardless of whether other candidates also file.
- If the ECI page is not updated by June 14, 2026, the question may resolve on the basis of clear, consistent reporting from credible Indian news outlets confirming whether James P.K. Sangma filed (or did not file) as the NPP candidate by the June 8 deadline.
- If the entire Meghalaya Rajya Sabha election is postponed or cancelled before the nomination deadline such that no nomination window closes by June 8, 2026, the question resolves NO.

factors.description: MDA-II coalition officially announced James P.K. Sangma as candidate on June 3, 2026 — formal decision already made
Filing was scheduled for today (June 5, 2026) at 2:30 PM — the act of filing is imminent or already done
3 more days remain until the June 8 deadline, providing ample buffer
Small residual risk of health emergency, sudden disqualification, or last-minute party reversal

input.anchor: Base rate: ~95% — once a ruling party officially announces a candidate for a Rajya Sabha seat they control, the candidate files nomination in virtually all cases
input.assessment: James P.K. Sangma has been officially announced as the MDA-II/NPP candidate on June 3, 2026. His nomination filing was scheduled for today (June 5) at 2:30 PM. The deadline is June 8. There is no credible reason to believe filing will not happen. The only NO scenarios involve extreme low-probability events (health emergency, sudden legal disqualification, party reversal after official announcement). This is essentially a near-certainty.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor is very high (~95%) because official announcements are almost never reversed for RS seats the party controls. Additional evidence (filing scheduled for today, 3-day buffer remaining) pushes even higher to ~97%. Not going above 97% due to residual black swan risk.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether the filing actually happened today as scheduled. Even if it didn't, 3 more days remain. The main uncertainty is vanishingly small tail risks.
input.tool_audit: research() was highly effective — returned comprehensive data from multiple credible Indian outlets (EastMojo, Times of India) confirming official announcement and filing schedule. get_metaculus_questions provided full question context. No tool failures.
input.process_reflection: This was a straightforward question where the research tool quickly surfaced the key fact (official announcement already made). The pipeline worked efficiently for this type of question where the answer is largely determined by a single confirmable fact.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Verification Summary

**Hallucinated evidence:** None found. All four factors are grounded in the research trace.

- **Factor 1** (MDA-II announcement on June 3): Confirmed by EastMojo and Times of India sources in the research results, with specific details about the meeting chaired by CM Conrad K. Sangma.
- **Factor 2** (Filing scheduled June 5 at 2:30 PM): Confirmed by the same research sources — "expected to file nomination papers on June 5, 2026 at 2:30 PM."
- **Factor 3** (3-day buffer): Straightforward arithmetic from the question's June 8 deadline and the forecast date.
- **Factor 4** (Residual risk): Not a factual claim — it's a reasonable tail-risk discounting factor.

**Double-counting:** Factors 1, 2, and 3 are related (announcement → scheduled filing → time buffer), but the agent explicitly acknowledges this in the trace: "The factors are partially overlapping (announcement and filing date are not independent — they're part of the same process), so my 97% is reasonable." The agent correctly adjusted downward from the ~99.7% that the raw logits would imply to 97%, accounting for the overlap. No error.

**Wrong-direction factors:** Factor 4 has logit=-0.3 (toward NO), correctly reflecting residual risk. All other factors point toward YES. ✅

**Resolution criteria alignment:** The question asks specifically whether James P.K. Sangma files as the NPP candidate by June 8. The factors directly engage with this: official NPP/MDA-II endorsement (Factor 1), scheduled filing act (Factor 2), and remaining time until deadline (Factor 3). The agent correctly notes that the question resolves YES even if the nomination is later withdrawn or rejected at scrutiny. ✅

**Missing resolution criteria:** Resolution criteria are fully provided and the agent fetched them via get_metaculus_questions. ✅

**Contradictory assessment:** The narrative assessment aligns with the factors — all point strongly to YES with only tail-risk scenarios for NO. ✅

**Counterargument quality:** The counterargument is adequate. It raises UDP's 12 seats and potential demand for their own candidate, possible legal disqualification, and the lack of confirmation that filing actually happened. While the agent then explains why these are unlikely, these are genuine alternative scenarios, not mere restatements of the conclusion.

**Confidence assessment:** 0.90 is reasonable given strong multi-source evidence with a small gap (no confirmation of actual filing, just that it was scheduled).

**Probability assessment:** Given the evidence — formal coalition announcement from June 3, filing scheduled for the very day of the forecast (June 5), 3-day buffer until deadline, no reported obstacles — I estimate ~96-98%. The agent's 97% sits squarely within this range. The anchor of 95% is appropriate (ruling party announcing a candidate → near-certain filing), and the +2pp departure is well-justified by the imminent filing schedule.

**Worldview consistency:** The superseded worldview entry (a6704725) used stale training data with approximate figures (NPP ~26 seats). The current fresh entry (e94904fd) correctly shows updated figures (NPP 33 seats, MDA-II 51 seats) consistent with the agent's factors. No contradictions.

**Tool audit:** Agent claims "No tool failures." Ground truth confirms 0 errors across 3 calls. ✅

No errors found.
